settings.title: Our Summer Courtyard Is Open Again
settings.excerpt: Twenty extra seats under the fig tree, plus a short warm-weather menu to match.
settings.body:
The weather has turned, which means the gate to the Saffron courtyard is unlocked once more.

There are twenty seats out back under the old fig tree, strung with lights for the evenings. It is first come, first served, so it fills quickly on warm nights.

The fig tree and the season

That fig tree has been in the courtyard longer than we have, and it does most of the work of making the space feel the way it does. By high summer its leaves throw real shade over the tables at lunch, and in the evening the lights strung through it take over. We do not take reservations for the courtyard, partly because the weather has the final say and partly because the best tables out there should be open to whoever turns up wanting them.

The menu shifts to suit the heat. We lean into dishes meant to be eaten slowly outdoors, the kind that go well with a long evening and a cold drink.

On the summer menu

  • A chilled yogurt soup to start, made for warm afternoons.
  • Grilled stone fruit with honey while it is in season.
  • Lighter plates throughout, suited to eating in the open air.

We have added a few lighter dishes for the season, including a chilled yogurt soup and grilled stone fruit with honey. The courtyard stays open through the first cool nights of autumn, so there is a good stretch ahead to enjoy it.
